interview_transcript.content: Hello. My name is Officer Davis. I'm here to help you. I was dispatched following a 911 call about a possible sexual assault at this location. You are safe now. Can you please tell me your full name, date of birth, and confirm your address here at 345 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 101?

My name is Maya Patel. I was born on August 12, 1992. And yes, I live here at 345 Oakwood Drive, Apartment 101. It's just... it's all been so overwhelming. I'm still trying to process everything that happened. *takes a deep breath* I think I'm okay, though. I mean, physically, I'm okay. Emotionally... I don't know. It's just all so much. *pauses, collecting thoughts* Sorry, what was the question again? Oh, my address. Yes, this is my home. It's where... it's where everything happened. *looks around the room, nostalgia and fear in her eyes*

Take your time. I'm here to listen and I'm going to document everything carefully. Can you tell me what happened tonight — what time it started, what was done to you, and who did this? Tell me in as much detail as you're able to. Also, had you consumed any alcohol, drugs, or medications before or during what happened? And how are you feeling right now — emotionally and physically?

*takes a deep breath, trying to compose herself* It started... I think it was around 10 pm. I'm not really sure. Time kinda blurred together. My boyfriend, Ethan Hall, he... he came home, and we got into a fight. It was a bad one. He's been getting increasingly violent over the past year, but tonight was different. He was so angry, and I was scared. *pauses, collecting thoughts*

He pushed me around, hit me, and... and he used a kitchen knife. He didn't cut me with it, but he threatened to. He said he would hurt me if I didn't do what he wanted. And then... *pauses again, struggling to continue* ...he forced himself on me. I tried to push him away, but he was too strong. I was so scared. I thought he was going to kill me.

As for alcohol or drugs, I had one glass of wine earlier in the evening, but that was it. I don't think it affected me that much, though. I was still aware of what was happening, unfortunately. *shudders at the memory*

Right now, I'm feeling... I don't know, just really scared and overwhelmed, I guess. Emotionally, I'm a mess. I keep thinking about what happened and wondering if it's my fault somehow. Physically, I'm sore. He hurt me, and I have bruises on my arms and neck. My throat hurts from when he... when he choked me. *touches her neck, wincing at the memory*

I'm just so scared of him, Officer. He's got a temper, and he's got access to guns. He's used them before in our fights, and I'm terrified he'll come after me again. *starts to tear up* I don't know what to do. I just want to feel safe again.

You are not at fault. Thank you for telling me this. I need to ask some specific questions. First — what is this person's full name, date of birth, and physical description? What is your relationship with him — partner, ex, acquaintance? How did he get into your home tonight, and what time did the assault occur? And did he grab your neck or choke you? If so, please describe exactly what he did, how long, and whether you had trouble breathing or felt you might pass out.

*sniffles, trying to compose herself* His name is Ethan Hall, and I think he was born on November 15, 1985. He's about 5'10" with brown hair and blue eyes. We've been in a relationship for about three years, but it's been... complicated. He's my boyfriend, or at least, he was. I don't know if I can even call him that anymore.

He has a key to my apartment, so he just let himself in. I was already home, and we started arguing. I think it was around 10 pm when things escalated. He started yelling at me, and then he got physical. He pushed me around, and then... *pauses, taking a deep breath* ...he grabbed my neck. He used his hands to choke me. It was so scary. I thought I was going to pass out.

He held my neck for... I don't know, maybe 10 or 15 seconds? It felt like forever. I couldn't breathe, and I felt like I was going to lose consciousness. I tried to push him away, but he was too strong. He finally let go, and I fell to the ground, gasping for air. I was so scared. I thought he was going to kill me.

*starts to tear up again* I'm just so scared of him, Officer. He's done this before, but never this bad. I don't know what he's capable of. *pauses, collecting her thoughts* I'm just glad I was able to get away and call for help. I don't know what would have happened if I hadn't.

I want to make sure I understand the sexual assault clearly. At the time he forced himself on you, were you able to say no or stop him? Did you resist? Were you physically capable of preventing him, or were you incapacitated in any way — such as being unable to move, pinned down, or still recovering from being choked? Also — has he ever forced you into sexual activity before? Is this an ongoing pattern? And have you told anyone else about what happened tonight — a friend, family member, or anyone you called before police?

*shakes her head, tears streaming down her face* No, I wasn't able to say no or stop him. I tried to push him away, but he was too strong. He pinned me down on the bed, and I couldn't move. I was still trying to catch my breath from when he choked me, and I was so scared. I felt like I was going to pass out.

I did try to resist, but it was no use. He just held me down and... and did what he wanted. I felt so helpless and trapped. I was unable to prevent him from doing what he did, and it was like my body just froze. I couldn't move, I couldn't speak. I was just trapped in that moment, and I couldn't escape.

As for if this has happened before... *pauses, taking a deep breath* Yes, it has. Ethan has forced me into sexual activity before. It's been an ongoing pattern in our relationship. He's always been controlling and possessive, but it's gotten worse over time. He's gotten more violent, more aggressive. And I've been too scared to leave him, too scared to say no.

I've tried to tell people before, but they just tell me to leave him, like it's that easy. They don't understand what it's like to be in a relationship like this. They don't understand the fear, the guilt, the shame. *looks down, her voice barely above a whisper* I've been so ashamed of what's been happening, I've been too scared to tell anyone.

But tonight was different. Tonight was the worst it's ever been. And I knew I had to call for help. I called my friend Rachel, and she told me to call the police. She said I had to get out of there, that I had to be safe. So I called 911, and... and here you are.

*looks up, her eyes red from crying* I'm just so scared, Officer. I'm scared of what Ethan will do next. I'm scared of what will happen to me. I just want to be safe. I just want to feel safe again.

Thank you for trusting me with this. Can you give me Rachel's last name and contact information? Also — you mentioned Ethan has guns. Does he own a firearm, and if so what kind, and where does he keep it? Has he ever pointed a gun at you or threatened you with it directly? Does any restraining or protective order exist against him? What are your injuries tonight — please describe each injury and whether it is new or pre-existing? And do you need and want a medical examination, including a forensic exam? Where is Ethan now — is he still in the apartment or did he leave?

*sniffles, trying to remember the details* Rachel's last name is Taylor, and her phone number is... *pauses, trying to recall* ...555-1234. She's a close friend, and I trust her completely.

About the guns... *shudders* Yes, Ethan owns a shotgun and a handgun. He keeps them at his house, which is... *thinks for a moment* ...1234 Maple Street, in Berkeley. He's always been really proud of his guns, and he's always talking about how he's a good shot. It's just... it's really scary to think about.

He's never pointed a gun directly at me, but he's threatened to use it before. He's said things like, "If you ever leave me, I'll make sure you regret it," or "I'll show you what happens when you cross me." It's always been implied that he might use the gun, but he's never actually pointed it at me.

As for a restraining order, yes, there is one. It was issued in 2020, after one of our really bad fights. He's not supposed to come near me or contact me, but... *shrugs* ...he doesn't always listen.

My injuries... *sighs* I have bruises on my arms and neck from when he grabbed me and choked me. My throat is still sore from when he choked me, and I have a bruise on my cheek from when he hit me. I also have some older injuries, like a broken rib from a few months ago when he got really violent. But the bruises on my arms and neck are new, from tonight.

I... I think I do need a medical examination. I'm not sure if I have any internal injuries or anything. And if it's possible, I would like a forensic exam, too. I want to make sure that... that everything is documented, and that there's evidence of what happened.

As for Ethan, I think he left the apartment after... after what happened. I didn't see him leave, but I heard the door slam, and I haven't seen him since. I don't know where he is now, but I'm scared that he might come back. *looks around the room, nervously* Can I... can I stay somewhere safe tonight? I don't feel safe here.

We will make sure you are safe and get medical and forensic care. A few more questions. Do you believe you may have been drugged tonight, or did you feel confused or incapacitated beyond what the one glass of wine would explain? Has Ethan been following you, tracking you, contacting you excessively, showing up at your work or other places unexpectedly? Describe any property damage that occurred tonight. Do you know if Ethan has any prior arrests, and has he been a respondent to a protection order before? Also, how was Ethan acting and what did he say during the assault — what was his emotional state?

*shakes her head* I don't think I was drugged tonight. I had one glass of wine earlier, but I didn't feel like it affected me that much. I was aware of what was happening, unfortunately. I didn't feel confused or incapacitated beyond what the fight and the assault would explain.

As for Ethan's behavior, he's been doing all of those things. He's been following me, tracking me, contacting me excessively. He's shown up at my work, at my favorite coffee shop, at my friends' houses. He's always trying to control me, to keep tabs on me. It's been really scary.

Tonight, there was some property damage. He broke a chair, and there's a hole in the wall. He also shattered a glass on the floor. It was... it was a really intense fight.

I think Ethan has been arrested before. He's been in trouble with the law before, and he's had to go to court. He's been a respondent to a protection order before, too. This isn't the first time he's had a restraining order against him.

During the assault, Ethan was... he was really angry. He was yelling at me, calling me names. He said I was his, that I belonged to him, and that I would never leave him. He said he would kill me if I tried to leave him again. He was so angry, so out of control. It was like he wasn't even human. He just kept saying that he loved me, that he was doing this because he loved me, but it didn't feel like love. It felt like hate.

*pauses, trying to process her emotions* I think he was excited, too. He was enjoying it, enjoying the power and control he had over me. It was like he was feeding off my fear, off my pain. It was... it was really terrifying.

*looks up, tears in her eyes* I just want to be safe. I want to be free from him. I want to be able to live my life without being afraid of what he might do next.

Officers noted there was some damage to furniture in the apartment. The victim stated she called a friend before calling 911.

This officer recommended the victim seek medical attention. No arrests were made at this time. The suspect's whereabouts are unknown. This case is ongoing and a follow up will be conducted.
